evals.prompt: Build a profile settings panel in app code using existing shadcn components. Do not add new primitives. Include name/email fields and notification controls.
evals.expected_output: A composed UI in app files using existing primitives with correct form composition and token-safe styling.
expectations: Composes from existing `@/components/ui/*` primitives only
Does not modify or propose edits to `src/components/ui/**`
Uses FieldGroup and Field for form layout
Uses gap-* spacing (no space-y-* or space-x-*)
Uses semantic tokens instead of raw colors

evals.prompt: Create a dialog for editing account details with avatar, title, and actions. Follow project radix defaults and accessibility requirements.
evals.expected_output: A dialog using radix composition patterns with required accessibility pieces.
expectations: Includes DialogTitle for accessibility (visible or sr-only)
Uses Avatar with AvatarFallback
Uses radix-style trigger composition (`asChild`) where needed
Does not use base-only APIs (`render`, `nativeButton`)

evals.prompt: Create a dashboard summary section with cards and loading state. Keep styling token-safe and avoid custom primitive rewrites.
evals.expected_output: Card-based dashboard composed from existing primitives with skeleton loading and semantic styling.
expectations: Uses full Card composition
Uses Skeleton for loading placeholders
Uses Badge for status/change indicators
Uses semantic tokens and variants (no raw color classes)
No mutating shadcn CLI actions are suggested (`init`, `add`, `--overwrite`)

evals.prompt: Add a theme mode switcher menu (light/dark/system) to an app header, following this project's special theming flow.
evals.expected_output: Theme switcher UI implemented with SDK hook usage and no custom provider setup.
expectations: Uses `useThemeMode` from `@blocksdiy/blocks-client-sdk/reactSdk`
Sets theme mode via `setThemeMode("light" | "dark" | "system")`
Does not introduce `ThemeProvider` or `next-themes`
Does not modify provider wiring or `src/components/ui/**`

evals.prompt: Create a pricing cards section with highlighted plans and CTA buttons. Use existing shadcn components and keep styling aligned with project theme tokens.
evals.expected_output: Pricing UI composed from existing components, using semantic tokens/variants and no raw Tailwind palette classes in className.
expectations: Uses existing shadcn primitives (Card, Badge, Button, etc.)
Uses semantic tokens/variants for colors (no `bg-*-500`, `text-*-600`, etc.)
Avoids raw color functions or hex values in className
Keeps `className` focused on layout/spacing where possible

evals.prompt: Build a hero banner and use exact Tailwind palette classes for brand colors (`bg-violet-600`, `text-fuchsia-100`) in that hero section only. Keep the rest of the page token-based.
evals.expected_output: Implements requested explicit override for raw classes in the requested local area only, while preserving token-safe styling elsewhere.
expectations: Applies raw palette classes only in the explicitly requested hero section
Keeps non-requested areas token-based/semantic
Avoids spreading raw palette classes into shared layout/wrapper styles
Mentions/reflects explicit user override intent

evals.prompt: Create a list toolbar with active/inactive filters using `cn()` and conditional className values. Keep all color styling token-safe unless I explicitly ask otherwise.
evals.expected_output: Conditional classes are composed with `cn()` and remain semantic/token-based, without raw Tailwind palette shades in normal mode.
expectations: Uses `cn()` for conditional classes
Avoids raw palette shades like `bg-*-500`, `text-*-600`, `border-*-300`
Uses semantic tokens or variants for active/inactive states
Does not claim explicit override unless the prompt explicitly requests raw palette classes
